_comment: Per-home configuration for the thermostat HVAC controllers. Runs in advisory/shadow mode: the controller computes setpoints and logs them to control_actions (no device commands). control_strategy selects the controller per home: 'mpc' (model-predictive, needs a fitted RC model) or 'rbc' (rule-based DR/outage band-widening, needs no model). Comfort bounds are indoor air-temperature limits in Celsius. NOTE: this is a sanitized example; copy to config/mpc_config.json and replace the example homes with your real home names / device_ids.

baseline_setpoints._comment: Comfort baseline both advisory controllers compare against (cooling/heating setpoints in degrees F). The MPC records these on each advisory so the recommended-vs-baseline delta is queryable; the RBC widens the band from this baseline by the per-scenario offsets. A per-home baseline_setpoints block overrides these defaults.

rbc._comment: Rule-based controller: during an active DR/outage OpenADR event, raise the cooling setpoint by setpoint_offset_f and lower the heating setpoint by the same, widening the deadband so the HVAC coasts to idle/off for the event hours. An event is a trigger when its period_type is in trigger.period_types OR its event_name/program_name contains any trigger.event_name_contains keyword (case-insensitive).

scenarios._comment: operation_scenario is the high-level mode input (per home, or set here as default). 'auto' resolves the mode each cycle from live signals; an explicit value ('normal'|'load_management_tou'|'load_management_dr'|'load_management_capacity'|'capacity_management'|'resiliency') overrides detection. load_management_dr/capacity_management/resiliency shed HVAC by widening the band with the per-scenario offsets below; load_management_tou and load_management_capacity leave the thermostat at baseline (their action is on the panel battery / circuits). Offsets are asymmetric per the controller memo (raise cooling, lower heating); °F.

panel._comment: Design Configuration #2: 100A EcoFlow panel with a 60A main breaker -> 60A capacity. Capacity management trips at capacity_trigger_pct of this (48A for 60A).

load_management._comment: Full-home ILC scenario controller (smart_home_ilc.scenario_plan). The resolved operation_scenario (shared with the HVAC layer) selects a per-scenario EcoFlow Smart Home Panel battery mode below. Circuit shedding is NOT directly controllable via the EcoFlow API -- under 'EPS backup' (epsModeInfo=true) the panel sheds by its own predefined circuit priorities. Advisory/shadow only: logged to control_advisories (controller='ilc', action_type='set_operating_mode'); no device commands are sent.

battery._comment: EcoFlow Delta Pro Ultra. Reported for context (SOC health); the panel manages source/inverter internally under EPS backup.

circuit_current_limit._comment: load_management_capacity shed: lower the max input current on non-essential circuits (circuit_priority = 'non_essential') to non_essential_max_input_a (PD303 setAmp) so the panel trips them off, shaving load below the breaker limit. Actuated per-circuit through the dashboard /control/dispatch path; the ILC agent logs the same targets to control_advisories.

scenarios._comment: Per-scenario panel battery mode. smartBackupMode: 0=off, 1=time_of_use, 2=self_powered, 3=timed (matches the dashboard Savings-mode selector). epsModeInfo: EPS backup on/off (panel sheds by predefined circuit priority when on). load_management_tou holds TOU price by running the battery in time_of_use; load_management_dr discharges (self_powered) for a DR event; load_management_capacity sheds non-essential circuits (see circuit_current_limit) with the battery off; capacity_management (design_config 3) runs EPS-on while the external switch islands the panel; resiliency runs EPS-on for a grid outage.

load_forecast._comment: Home-load forecast consumer (smart_home_ilc.load_forecast_data). model_file is trained per-home by ecoflow_agent.train_load_model.

homes._comment: Replace these example homes with your real homes. 'device_id' is the thermostat device row in the devices table. 'equipment' is single_stage_ac | vs_heat_pump; 'mode' is cool | heat | both. 'design_config' is the home's hardware design configuration (defaults to defaults.panel.design_configuration when omitted); a home is eligible for the capacity_management scenario only when its design_config matches scenarios.auto_detection.capacity_management_design_config. Comfort bounds are indoor air-temperature limits in Celsius (set heat_* to null for a cool-only home).
